---
name: nexusmotion-uiux
description: "Use when designing Blazor UI/UX for Nexus Motion, including component layout, responsive behavior, accessibility criteria, interaction patterns, and UI-to-QA acceptance criteria handoff."
---

# Nexus Motion UI/UX Skill

## When To Use

- Building or refining page/component UI.
- Defining responsive layout behavior.
- Defining accessibility expectations and interaction states.

## Required Inputs

- User story or feature request.
- Current component/page context.
- Constraints: device targets, brand direction, and timeline.

## Process

1. Define user intent and success criteria.
2. Propose layout and interaction model for desktop and mobile.
3. Specify accessibility requirements (keyboard, focus, semantics, contrast).
4. Map design behavior to Blazor component boundaries.
5. Produce QA handoff artifacts.

## Required Outputs

- UI behavior summary.
- Responsive rules.
- Accessibility checklist.
- Acceptance criteria for QA.
- Assumptions and unresolved risks.

## Verification Checklist

- Includes mobile and desktop behavior.
- Includes accessibility checks.
- Criteria are testable and unambiguous.
- Handoff to QA is explicit.

## Non-Goals

- Making architecture-layer decisions.
- Writing full test implementations.
